insights.headline: Category leader despite Adyen's recent enterprise wins
insights.evidence: Across 2025-2026 G2 segment data, Stripe still leads SMB and mid-market payments by margin >2x while Adyen leads enterprise. The two have stopped overlapping.

insights.headline: Bridge acquisition (Oct 2024) is the bet on stablecoin rails
insights.evidence: Stripe acquired Bridge to own crypto-fiat conversion. The strategy: don't compete on stablecoins, route them.

insights.headline: Self-serve up to mid-tier; everything above is opaque
insights.evidence: Pricing page shows per-transaction up to standard plan; enterprise tier requires contact sales with no anchor pricing. Reviews mention long sales cycles for the larger deals.

raw_facts: AE roles concentrated in NY, SF, Dublin
gaps: could not confirm AE/SE ratio without LinkedIn API

insights.headline: Sessions API launched last quarter — first new dev primitive in 18mo
insights.evidence: The Sessions API replaces multi-step Checkout/PaymentIntent dance for a flat session-based UX. First major surface change since 2024.

insights.headline: Issuing + Treasury increasingly co-positioned — embedded-finance push
insights.evidence: The /products page groups Issuing and Treasury under 'Banking-as-a-Service' for the first time. Prior org had them as separate pillars.

insights.headline: Engineering blog post 'Ruby at scale: 10 years of monolith pragmatism'
insights.evidence: Stripe wrote publicly about staying on Ruby/Rails through scale, with Sorbet type-checking as the layer that made it work. Counter-narrative to the microservices default.

raw_facts: Stack: Ruby/Rails + Sorbet, Go for some services
gaps: Wappalyzer would give more frontend stack detail

insights.headline: Owns the 'how to accept payments' SEO category outright
insights.evidence: Top 3 results for queries like 'how to accept credit cards online', 'payment gateway api', 'subscription billing setup' are Stripe docs / blog. They've made docs the funnel.

raw_facts: Docs estimated to drive >40% of new sign-ups (per Patrick Collison interview)

insights.headline: CFO Steffan Tomlinson joined from Confluent — IPO playbook in motion
insights.evidence: Tomlinson took Palo Alto Networks and Confluent public; joined Stripe Dec 2024. Pattern is unmistakable.

raw_facts: Co-founders Patrick + John Collison both still active

insights.headline: 85% of open eng roles are L5+; almost no junior pipeline
insights.evidence: Careers filter: 47 senior/staff/principal roles, 8 mid, 0 junior or new-grad. At Stripe's headcount, this concentrates institutional knowledge in too few people.

insights.headline: Logo wall shows F500; G2 segment skews 60% small business
insights.evidence: Homepage lists Amazon, Microsoft, Google, Salesforce. G2 reviewer segment data: 60% SMB, 28% mid-market, 12% enterprise. The enterprise wins are real but a minority of the actual reviewer base.

raw_facts: Logos: Amazon, Microsoft, Google, Salesforce, Shopify, OpenAI

insights.headline: $91.5B tender at $70B implied valuation — flat-to-up despite ZIRP unwind
insights.evidence: Per Reuters Feb 2025, secondary tender priced at ~$70B vs prior peak $95B. Flat valuation through a brutal correction; investors are repricing rather than fleeing.

raw_facts: Last raise: 2023 Series I, $6.5B at $50B valuation
gaps: headcount estimate from Money domain (8500) differs from People (8000)
